description: City employee base salary as of 2020-12-31. Starting with 2020 data, in an effort to provide greater consumable and comparable data we're moving away from reporting individual employee earnings and instead provide base salaries as assigned per position. Due to this change this dataset cannot be directly combined with previous years' earnings data. The data represents a snapshot of salaries as of 2020-12-31 rather than actual paid amounts in 2020. Personnel spending data can be found on openbook.

description: Annual City employee earnings for calendar-year 2017. Earnings figures are those taxed by Medicare. This dataset cannot be directly combined with previous- or future- year earnings because it doesn't account for title changes or other job status changes. Further, the data represents paid amounts rather than stated salaries – i.e. if an employee worked March-December of a given year, the data reflects 10 months of compensation for the year.

columns.description: Units: inches. Incremental rainfall is the amount of rainfall measured just before the data are sent.

description: Automated readings from the rain gages throughout the city. These rain gages use 'tipping buckets' to measure rainfall; whenever the 'bucket' tips, the incremental rainfall data are recorded. Because of this measurement system, this dataset is only updated when there are new 'bucket tips'; the system searches for new data several times an hour, but only updates this dataset if there are new records. The data rate is limited, so for heavy rainfall there will be multiple 'bucket tips' in one recor

columns.name: Rate Change Notes
columns.description: Refers to the following for notes about the specified rate change. 1: The intent of the 2003 water rate increase was to collect 6% increase in revenue with a 15% reduction in use. Increases by rate class were shown with no change in use. 2: Single family and duplex residendital water rates were adjusted May 1, 2004 to a 4 tier rate with lower costs per gallon because it was anticipated that water usage would increase. 3: Single family and duplex residendital water rates were adjusted May 1, 2006 to a 3 tier rate. 4: Approximate value.

description: Automated readings from the city-wide flood warning system, which is made up in part of stream-flow gages throughout the city. While these data specify flow rates at each gage, extreme care should be taken if using these measurements as flow data; the sensors are not placed ideally for accurate flow measurements and they are not monitored closely enough for this purpose. This is the raw sensor data and analysis would be needed to use for anything but flood monitoring. The real-time map of these

name: Fort Collins UCR Crime Totals

columns.name: Robbery
columns.description: The taking or attempting to take anything of value from the care, custody, or control of a person or persons by force or threat of force or violence and/or by putting the victim in fear.

columns.name: Burglary
columns.description: The unlawful entry of a structure to commit a felony or a theft (includes attempted forcible entry)

columns.name: Rape
columns.description: Penetration, no matter how slight, of the vagina or anus with any body part or object, or oral penetration by a sex organ of another person, without the consent of the victim

columns.name: Homicide
columns.description: The willful (non-negligent) killing of one human being by another

columns.name: Larceny/Theft
columns.description: The unlawful taking, carrying, leading, or riding away of property from the possession or constructive possession of another (excludes embezzlement, confidence games, forgery, check fraud, etc.)

columns.name: Assault-Aggravated
columns.description: An unlawful attack by one person upon another for the purpose of inflicting severe or aggravated bodily injury. This type of assault is usually accompanied by the use of a weapon or by other means likely to produce death or great bodily harm

columns.name: meta_row_id
columns.description: Metadata field for record hash. This field is a SHA-1 hash of all other fields in the record, used for validation of record uniqueness.

columns.name: meta_row_index
columns.description: Metadata field for a suggested sort order. Sort this column in ascending order to order the dataset the way it was intended from the source system.

columns.name: Motor Vehicle Theft
columns.description: The theft or attempted theft of a motor vehicle

columns.name: Arson
columns.description: Any willful or malicious burning or attempting to burn, with or without intent to defraud, a dwelling house, public building, motor vehicle or aircraft, personal property of another, etc

description: These data are for the the FBI Uniform Crime Reporting (UCR) index. The crime index includes Willful Homicide, Rape, Robbery, Aggravated Assault, Burglary, Larceny (Theft), Motor Vehicle Theft, and Arson (definitions are in each column header). State-wide crime information can be found at the Colorado Bureau of Investigation (https://www.colorado.gov/cbi).

name: Daily Fort Collins OpenData Portal Analytics

columns.name: ga:sessions
columns.description: From Google Analytics API: the total number of sessions

columns.name: endDate
columns.description: Inclusive end date for analytics capture

columns.name: url
columns.description: URL of a specific page, or all for all pages in the domain

columns.name: assetTitle
columns.description: The title of the data asset, when a single asset is referenced in the URL

columns.name: ga:avgSessionDuration
columns.description: From Google Analytics API: the average duration (in seconds) of users sessions

columns.name: ga:hits
columns.description: From Google Analytics API: total number of hits for the view (profile). This metric sums all hit types, including pageview, custom event, ecommerce, and other types. Because this metric is based on the view (profile), not on the property, it is not the same as the propertys hit volume

columns.name: ga:newUsers
columns.description: From Google Analytics API: the number of sessions marked as a user's first session. This resets each time period

columns.name: startDate
columns.description: Inclusive start date for analytics capture

columns.name: ga:users
columns.description: From Google Analytics API: the total number of users for the requested time period

columns.name: ga:sessionDuration
columns.description: From Google Analytics API: total duration (in seconds) of users sessions

columns.name: ga:avgTimeOnPage
columns.description: From Google Analytics API: the average time users spent viewing this page or a set of pages

columns.name: ga:percentNewSessions
columns.description: From Google Analytics API: the percentage of sessions by users who had never visited the property before

columns.name: meta_row_index
columns.description: Metadata field for a suggested sort order. Sort this column in ascending order to order the dataset the way it was intended from the source system.

columns.name: ga:pageviewsPerSession
columns.description: From Google Analytics API: the average number of pages viewed during a session, including repeated views of a single page

columns.name: ga:pageviews
columns.description: From Google Analytics API: the total number of pageviews for the property

columns.name: ga:bounceRate
columns.description: From Google Analytics API: the percentage of single-page session (i.e., session in which the person left the property from the first page)

columns.name: ga:bounces
columns.description: From Google Analytics API: the total number of single page (or single interaction hit) sessions for the property

columns.name: meta_row_id
columns.description: Metadata field for record hash. This field is a SHA-1 hash of all other fields in the record, used for validation of record uniqueness.

columns.name: ga:timeOnPage
columns.description: From Google Analytics API: time (in seconds) users spent on a particular page, calculated by subtracting the initial view time for a particular page from the initial view time for a subsequent page. This metric does not apply to exit pages of the property

description: Daily data from the Google Analytics tag for the Open Data Portal at OpenData.fcgov.com. Analytics shown are presumed to be non-City-employees, as these data come from computers external to the City network. Each day starting at the first day for which there are data is included, and the URL is either a specific page or "all", specifying that every page in the domain is included. Specific-page URLs are filtered to the main Portal page or data assets, so "all" may capture more pages than specifie

columns.name: DATE
columns.description: Date of measurement

columns.description: Total water flow into the Mulberry and Drake reclamation plants, in millions of gallons per day.

description: Time series of daily City of Fort Collins wastewater in need of reclamation, reported in 'millions of gallons per day' (MGD). Note that this is provisional data unless otherwise specified.

name: 2016 Fort Collins City Employee Earnings

columns.name: meta_row_index
columns.description: Metadata field for a suggested sort order. Sort this column in ascending order to order the dataset the way it was intended from the source system.

columns.name: meta_row_id
columns.description: Metadata field for record hash. This field is a SHA-1 hash of all other fields in the record, used for validation of record uniqueness.

description: Annual City employee earnings for calendar-year 2016. Earnings figures are those taxed by Medicare. This dataset cannot be directly combined with previous- or future- year earnings because it doesn't account for title changes or other job status changes. Further, the data represents paid amounts rather than stated salaries – i.e. if an employee worked March-December of a given year, the data reflects 10 months of compensation for the year.

name: Daily Fort Collins OpenBook Analytics

columns.name: ga:bounceRate
columns.description: From Google Analytics API: the percentage of single-page session (i.e., session in which the person left the property from the first page)

columns.description: From Google Analytics API: total duration (in seconds) of users sessions

columns.description: From Google Analytics API: the average time users spent viewing this page or a set of pages

columns.description: Inclusive end date for analytics capture

columns.name: ga:hits
columns.description: From Google Analytics API: total number of hits for the view (profile). This metric sums all hit types, including pageview, custom event, ecommerce, and other types. Because this metric is based on the view (profile), not on the property, it is not the same as the propertys hit volume

columns.description: From Google Analytics API: time (in seconds) users spent on a particular page, calculated by subtracting the initial view time for a particular page from the initial view time for a subsequent page. This metric does not apply to exit pages of the property

description: Daily data from the Google Analytics tag for OpenBook at OpenBook.fcgov.com. Analytics shown are presumed to be non-City-employees, as these data come from computers external to the City network. Each day starting at the first day for which there are data is included, and the URL is either a specific page or "all", specifying that every page in the domain is included. Specific-page URLs are filtered to the main Portal page or data assets, so "all" may capture more pages than specified individual

columns.name: Rescue Mission # of Nights with Turnaways - Women

description: This dataset shows data on the numbers served by Catholic Charities and Fort Collins Rescue Mission shelters in Fort Collins. It is updated monthly with the previous-month"s data. These data are recorded by the shelters and compiled by the City of Fort Collins. To note when using this data: the Rescue Mission does not have family rooms or accept children, and Catholic Charities does not report turnaways by gender.

description: Annual City employee earnings for calendar-year 2010. Earnings figures are those taxed by Medicare. This dataset cannot be directly combined with previous- or future- year earnings because it doesn't account for title changes or other job status changes. Further, the data represents paid amounts rather than stated salaries – i.e. if an employee worked March-December of a given year, the data reflects 10 months of compensation for the year.

name: 2023 Fort Collins City Employee Base Pay

columns.name: meta_row_id
columns.description: Metadata field for record hash. This field is a SHA-1 hash of all other fields in the record, used for validation of record uniqueness.

columns.name: meta_row_index
columns.description: Metadata field for a suggested sort order. Sort this column in ascending order to order the dataset the way it was intended from the source system.

description: City employee base salary as of 2023-12-31. Starting with 2020 data, in an effort to provide greater consumable and comparable data we're moving away from reporting individual employee earnings and instead provide base salaries as assigned per position. Due to this change this dataset cannot be directly combined with previous years' earnings data. The data represents a snapshot of salaries as of 2023-12-31 rather than actual paid amounts in 2023. Personnel spending data can be found on openbook.

name: Fort Collins Water Rate History (with use reduction)

columns.name: Flat
columns.description: Residential customers that use the flat rate structure.

columns.name: Effective Date
columns.description: Date the rate change went into effect.

columns.name: Rate Change Notes
columns.description: Refers to the following for notes about the specified rate change. 1: The intent of the 2003 water rate increase was to collect 6% increase in revenue with a 15% reduction in use. Increases by rate class were shown with no change in use. 2: Single family and duplex residendital water rates were adjusted May 1, 2004 to a 4 tier rate with lower costs per gallon because it was anticipated that water usage would increase. 3: Single family and duplex residendital water rates were adjusted May 1, 2006 to a 3 tier rate. 4: Approximate value.
Commercial customers with specified size input line service.

name: 2026 Fort Collins City Employee Base Pay
description: City employee base salary as of 1/13/2026. The 2026 data provides base salaries for every position's compensation. The data represents a snapshot of base salaries for all employees as of 1/13/2026 for the City of Fort Collins in an hourly rate or annual salary format for 2026 rather than a forecasted amount of actual salary payments for 2026. Personnel spending data can be found on openbook.fcgov.com
